content_en:
“You will not enter Paradise until you believe, and you will not

believe until you love one another. Shall I not guide you to

something, which, if you do it, will make you love one another? Spread greetings of peace among yourselves.’"]

And in yet another hadith, the Messenger of Allah #8 said,

(OE Gail 3425 0 BA GG ee pate ple oss (SydodI oat

“The rights of a Muslim upon another Muslim are six.” He 2% was then asked, ‘‘And what are they, O’ Messenger of Allah?” He #8 said, “If you meet a Muslim, then extend to him greetings of peace...’"1

The Manners Of Giving Greetings Of Peace

1) It ls Sunnah To Initiate Greetings Of Peace, It Is Waajib

(Compulsory) To Return The Greetings

There are many proofs which show that it is Sunnah to initiate

"1 Bukhaaree (3326), Muslim (2841)

"1 Related by Muslim, in the chapter, “None Shall Enter Paradise Except For

greetings of peace. We have just mentioned one of those proofs:

“The rights of a Muslim upon another ‘Muslim are six. Df you meet a Muslim, then extend to him greetings of peace.

Another proof is the practice of the Prophet #%, or of his Companions 4; we will not mention proofs to that effect because their practice of extending greetings of peace to one another is so well known.

If person A extends greetings of peace to person B, the latter must return the greetings; otherwise, he is sinning. Among the many proofs that establish this ruling, is the saying of Allah 38:

Par 2 At rad 2 gosh iss thee oc Koi, Ach sp

“When you are greeted with a resting, greet in return with what is better than it, or (at least) return it equally” (Qur’an 4:86)

Tbn Hazm, Ibn ‘Abdul-Barr, and Ash-Shaikh Taqee-ud-Deen - each of these have said that there is a consensus among the scholars in this issue: that it is compulsory to return greetings of peace. Question: If a man greets a group of people, does each one of them have to respond to his greeting, or is it sufficient for one of them to respond?

Answer: If a man greets a group of people, it is better for all of them to respond; however, if only one of them returns the greeting, the others do not have to return it as well, and there is no sin upon them for not returning it. ‘Alee Ibn Abee Taalib 4 said, ‘When a group passes by, it is sufficient for one of them to give greetings of peace, and as for those who are sitting, it is sufficient for one of them to respond.”

"I Related by Abu Daawood (5210). Al-Albaanee said, “sahih.” And Ibn ‘Abdul-Barr related it with his chain that goes back to the Prophet # and described it as being Hasan, without anything that opposes it. However, Ibn ‘Abdul-Barr said the following about Sa’eed Ibn Khaalid AlKhuzaa’ee, one of the narrators: “Some do not find any fault with him,

Chapter -2 The Manners Of Giving Greetings Of Peace 57

2) The Manner Of Giving Greetings Of Peace

In order of best, second best, and third best, these are the ways of extending greetings of peace:

1) “Assalaamu ‘Alaikum Warahmatullahi Wabarakaatuhu”’: Peace as well as the mercy and blessings of Allah upon you.

2) “Asslaamu ‘Alaikum Warahmatullah’: Peace and the mercy of Allah upon you.

3) “Assalamu ‘Alaikum’’: Peace upon you.

This order is proven by the narration of Abu Hurairah 4, in which a man passed by the Messenger of Allah 2% - who was seated in a gathering - and said, “Assalaamu ‘Alaikum.” The Prophet # said, “Ten good deeds [are written for him].” Another man passed by and said, “Assalaamu ‘Alaikum Warahmatullah,” and the Prophet #8 said, “Twenty good deeds.” Then another man passed by and said, ““Assalaamu ‘Alaikum Warahmatullahi Wabarakaatuhu,” and the Prophet #% said, ‘Thirty good deeds,"

I mentioned the ways of extending greetings of peace from best to least best, and in responding to one of those greetings, one should give a similar or better greeting, for Allah 3€ said:

but a group [of scholars] have ruled that he is weak.” (At-Tamheed (6:290). And in Al-Irwaa Al-Ghaleel, Ash-Shaikh Al-Albaanee ruled that the hadith is Hasan, and he mentioned the ruling of An-Naisaabooree: “This hadith is Hasan.” He then mentioned the different chains for this hadith and their overall effect of strengthening the hadith, and at the end of his commentary, he said, “With its various chains, perhaps this hadith becomes stronger, to the level of Hasan; and this seems to be the stronger view, and Allah knows best (Al-Irwaa (778).” Note: I have gone in detail here because it is necessary to know if there is a sin on a group of people if only one of them responds to a greeting, and the ruling in this issue hinges upon the authenticity of this narration.

" Related by At-Tirmidhee (2689), who said, “The hadith is Hasan sahih, and Ghareeb from this chain.” And Al-Bukhaaree related it in Al-Adab AlMufrad (986). Al-Albaanee said, “sahih.” Ad-Daarimee (264) and Ahmad (19446) related it as well.

“When you are greeted with a greeting, greet in return with what is better than it, or (at least) return it equally’ (Quran 4:86)

And even if you are responding to the greeting of a single Muslim, you should use the plural pronoun: ‘Wa-‘AlaikumusSalaam...’ instead of ‘Wa-‘Alaikas-Salaam.’

Question: Suppose someone gives us greetings of peace and ends his greeting with, ‘Wabarakaatuhu.’ In order to respond with a better greeting, as is mentioned in the verse

may we add, ‘Wa-maghfiratuhu Wa-Ihsaanuhu...(And His forgiveness and generosity...)?

Answer: When responding to another person’s greeting, you should not add anything after, ““Wabarakaatuhu,” even if that is where the other person ended. Ibn ‘Abdul-Barr said, “Ibn ‘Abbaas # and Ibn ‘Umar # said to, ‘Finish [greetings of] peace with Al-Barakah (i.e., ‘Wabarakaatuhu’), just as Allah 3% said about His righteous slaves:

“The Mercy of Allah and His Blessings be on you, O the family [of Ibraaheem]”’ (Quran 11:73).

And they both disliked for anyone to add anything after ‘Wabarakaatuhu’ in his greetings of peace.”

3) It Is Disliked To Initiate A Greeting With “Alaikas-Salaam (Thus Reversing The Word Order, Which Is Correctly Done When One Responds To Another Person’s Greeting)”

There are authentic narrations that establish this ruling; for

example, Jaabir Ibn Saleem Al-Hujaimee 4 said, ‘I went to the

Prophet #% and said, ‘Alaikus-Salaam,’ to which he #% responded,

Chapter -2 The Manners Of Giving Greetings Of Peace 59

‘Do no say, ‘Alaikus-Salaam,’ but instead say, ‘As-Salaamu- ‘Alaik.”"

And the wording of the Prophet's answer is slightly different in Abu Daawood’s narration:

“Do not say, ‘Alaikus-Salaam,’ for ‘Alaikus-Salaam,’ is the greeting of the dead.’

Therefore, it is disliked to initiate a greeting with, “AlaikusSalaam,”

4) If You Are Greeting A Large Gathering Or If You Doubt Whether The Person You Greeted Heard You, It Is Recommended To Repeat The Greeting Three Times

5) It Is Sunnah To Initiate Greetings And Respond To Them Out Loud

It is from the guidance of the Prophet # that he would raise his voice when he gave and returned greetings of peace. One does

"I Sunan Abu Daawood (5209); Al-Albaanee said, “sahih.””

not, therefore, receive reward for whispering greetings or saying them in such a low voice that no one but he can hear them - there is an exception to this ruling, which we will come to later on. Thaabit Ibn ‘Ubaid said, “I went to a gathering wherein ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Umar was present, and he said, ‘When you give greetings of peace, then let others hear you, for it is a blessed and good greeting.””"1)

Ibn Hajr pointed out that the Prophet 2 ordered Muslims to spread greetings of peace among themselves, but that order cannot be fulfilled when one gives greetings in a low, inaudible voice; hence, in order for one to comply with that command, one must initiate and return greetings in a loud voice. Consequently, it is not enough for one to signal his greetings with, for instance, a motion of the hand.

And An-Nawawee ruled that one who gives an inaudible greeting of peace has given no greeting whatsoever; hence, it is not compulsory - nor really possible - for the other person to respond. At the very least, one should return greetings, if not out loud, then at least in an audible tone; otherwise, he has not fulfilled the compulsory duty of responding to greetings of peace.

6) It Is Sunnah To Make One’s Greetings Inclusive Of Everyone — Of Those One Knows And Of Those One Does Not Know”!

A man once asked the Prophet #, ‘Which Islam is best?” The Prophet 2 said, W535 AS ay Cie U4 Je pol 1s pda Bb “Feeding food [to others] and saying ‘As-Salaam,’ both to those you know and to those you don’t know.)

"1 Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (1005); Al-Albaanee said, “It has an authentic chain.” And Al-Haafidh said the same (11/18): sahih Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (pg. 385)

2] Of course, this ruling applies in the case of Muslims and not in the case of disbelievers, for they are not befitting of this supplication.

'9] Al-Bukhaaree (12) and Muslim (39)

Chapter -2 The Manners Of Giving Greetings Of Peace 61
content_id:
"Kalian tidak akan masuk surga hingga kalian beriman, dan kalian tidak akan beriman hingga kalian saling mencintai. Maukah kalian aku tunjukkan sesuatu yang jika kalian melakukannya, akan membuat kalian saling mencintai? Sebarkanlah salam di antara kalian."

Dan dalam hadis lain, Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Hak seorang Muslim atas Muslim lainnya ada enam." Kemudian beliau ditanya: "Apa sajakah itu, wahai Rasulullah?" Beliau bersabda: "Jika kamu bertemu seorang Muslim, ucapkanlah salam kepadanya..."

Adab Memberi Salam

1) Disunahkan Memulai Salam; Wajib Membalas Salam

Terdapat banyak dalil yang menunjukkan bahwa memulai salam adalah Sunnah. Kita telah menyebutkan salah satunya: "Hak seorang Muslim atas Muslim lainnya ada enam. Jika kamu bertemu seorang Muslim, ucapkanlah salam kepadanya."

Jika seseorang (A) mengucapkan salam kepada seseorang (B), yang terakhir wajib membalas salam tersebut; jika tidak, ia berdosa. Di antara banyak dalil yang menetapkan hukum ini adalah firman Allah: "Apabila kamu diberi penghormatan dengan sesuatu penghormatan, maka balaslah penghormatan itu dengan yang lebih baik atau balaslah dengan yang serupa." (Al-Quran 4:86)

Ibn Hazm, Ibn 'Abdul-Barr, dan Syaikh Taqiyuddin — masing-masing dari mereka mengatakan bahwa terdapat ijma di antara para ulama dalam masalah ini: bahwa wajib membalas salam.

Pertanyaan: Jika seorang laki-laki mengucapkan salam kepada sekelompok orang, apakah setiap orang dari mereka harus membalas salamnya, atau sudah cukup jika salah satu dari mereka membalas?

Jawaban: Jika seorang laki-laki mengucapkan salam kepada sekelompok orang, lebih baik jika semua dari mereka membalas; namun jika hanya satu orang yang membalas, yang lain tidak harus membalas dan tidak ada dosa bagi mereka. 'Ali Ibn Abi Thalib berkata: "Jika suatu rombongan melewati, cukup bagi salah seorang dari mereka untuk mengucapkan salam; dan bagi yang duduk, cukup bagi salah seorang dari mereka untuk menjawab."

2) Cara Memberi Salam

Urutan dari yang terbaik, kedua terbaik, dan ketiga terbaik, berikut adalah cara-cara mengucapkan salam:

1) "Assalamu'alaikum warahmatullahi wabarakatuh": Semoga keselamatan serta rahmat dan berkah Allah terlimpah atas kalian.

2) "Assalamu'alaikum warahmatullah": Semoga keselamatan dan rahmat Allah terlimpah atas kalian.

3) "Assalamu'alaikum": Semoga keselamatan terlimpah atas kalian.

Urutan ini dibuktikan oleh riwayat Abu Hurairah, di mana seorang laki-laki melewati Rasulullah ﷺ — yang sedang duduk dalam sebuah pertemuan — dan berkata: "Assalamu'alaikum." Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Sepuluh kebaikan (dicatat untuknya)." Laki-laki lain melewati dan berkata: "Assalamu'alaikum warahmatullah," dan Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Dua puluh kebaikan." Kemudian laki-laki lain melewati dan berkata: "Assalamu'alaikum warahmatullahi wabarakatuh," dan Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Tiga puluh kebaikan."

Aku menyebutkan cara-cara mengucapkan salam dari yang terbaik hingga yang kurang baik, dan dalam membalas salah satu salam tersebut, seseorang hendaknya memberikan salam yang serupa atau lebih baik, sebagaimana Allah berfirman: "Apabila kamu diberi penghormatan dengan sesuatu penghormatan, maka balaslah penghormatan itu dengan yang lebih baik atau balaslah dengan yang serupa." (Al-Quran 4:86)

3) Dimakruhkan Memulai Salam dengan "'Alaika As-Salam" (Membalik Urutan Kata)

Terdapat riwayat-riwayat sahih yang menetapkan hukum ini. Jabir Ibn Salim Al-Hujaimi berkata: "Aku mendatangi Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: ''Alaika As-Salam,' dan beliau menjawab: 'Jangan ucapkan "'Alaika As-Salam," tetapi ucapkanlah "As-Salamu 'Alaik."'"

Dalam riwayat Abu Dawud: "Jangan ucapkan ''Alaika As-Salam,' karena ''Alaika As-Salam' adalah salamnya orang mati."

4) Jika Anda Memberi Salam kepada Sekelompok Besar atau Jika Anda Meragukan apakah Orang yang Anda Salami Mendengar Anda, Dianjurkan untuk Mengulang Salam Tiga Kali

5) Disunahkan Memulai Salam dan Membalasnya dengan Suara yang Jelas

Termasuk dari petunjuk Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau meninggikan suaranya ketika memberi dan membalas salam. Ibn Hajr menunjukkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan kaum Muslim untuk menyebarkan salam di antara mereka, namun perintah itu tidak dapat terlaksana ketika seseorang mengucapkan salam dengan suara rendah yang tidak terdengar orang lain. Oleh karena itu, tidaklah cukup memberi isyarat salam dengan gerakan tangan saja.

6) Disunahkan Menjadikan Salam Bersifat Inklusif — Mencakup Orang yang Dikenal maupun yang Tidak Dikenal

Seorang laki-laki pernah bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ: "Islam manakah yang terbaik?" Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Memberi makan (kepada orang lain) dan mengucapkan 'As-Salam,' baik kepada yang kamu kenal maupun yang tidak kamu kenal."
content_ar:
«لا تدخلوا الجنة حتى تؤمنوا، ولا تؤمنوا حتى تحابوا، أولا أدلكم على شيء إذا فعلتموه تحاببتم؟ أفشوا السلام بينكم».

وفي حديث آخر قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم:

«حق المسلم على المسلم ست». فقيل: وما هي يا رسول الله؟ قال: «إذا لقيته فسلّم عليه، وإذا دعاك فأجبه، وإذا استنصحك فانصح له، وإذا عطس فحمد الله فشمّته، وإذا مرض فعُده، وإذا مات فاتبعه». رواه مسلم.

وهذه الحقوق الست تُجسّد منظومة الترابط الاجتماعي في الإسلام؛ فهي تشمل التحية والاستجابة والنصيحة وتشميت العاطس وعيادة المريض وتشييع الجنازة. وكل حق من هذه الحقوق يمثّل جانبًا من جوانب الأخوة الإسلامية الحقيقية التي تتجاوز مجرد العلاقة الشكلية إلى المشاركة الوجدانية الفعلية.

وقد حثّ الإسلام على المصافحة عند اللقاء لما فيها من إذهاب الوحشة وتوطيد الألفة؛ وقد ثبت في الحديث أن المتصافحَين لا يفترقان حتى يُغفر لهما. ومن آداب المصافحة: أن تكون باليدين معًا في بعض الروايات، وإن كانت المصافحة بيد واحدة أيضًا ثابتة. ويُكره التقبيل والانحناء للتعظيم عند جمهور أهل العلم.
